document_metadata.title: Complete Source Material for Atlas/Quantum Forge Cinematic Pipeline
document_metadata.purpose: Story-first automation and director-style meta-learning implementation

characters.name: Eleanor Vance
characters.description: Anxious graduate student researching paranormal phenomena, invited as 'subject expert'
characters.backstory: Saw her first ghost at age 7, spent two decades trying to rationalize it away through academic study
characters.goals: Prove or disprove the supernatural scientifically, survive the weekend
characters.conflicts: Her gifts make her vulnerable to the house's influence

visual_markers.physical: Pale skin, dark circles under eyes from insomnia, messy auburn hair always in practical bun
visual_markers.costume: Practical layers - turtleneck, cardigan, jeans, hiking boots
visual_markers.signature_items: Wire-frame glasses, leather journal, EMF detector

quote_examples: "I've spent twenty years trying to explain this away"

characters.name: Marcus Sterling
characters.description: Investigative journalist writing exposé on 'haunted house tourism scams'
characters.backstory: Lost credibility on a story, needs this exposé to rebuild career
characters.goals: Expose the Blackwood 'haunting' as fraud, find the con
characters.conflicts: Evidence contradicts his skepticism, past trauma resurfaces

visual_markers.physical: Mixed race (Black/Asian), athletic build, well-groomed beard, tired eyes
visual_markers.costume: Smart casual - button-down shirt, dark jeans, leather jacket, boots

characters.wardrobe_notes: Layers suggest readiness to leave quickly, practical over stylish
characters.motivation: Redemption through truth-telling, even if truth is terrifying

characters.name: Victoria Ashford
characters.description: Descendant of the original Blackwood family, inherited the estate
characters.secret: Her great-grandfather committed the murders that cursed the house
characters.motivation: Trying to break the curse before it claims her

visual_markers.physical: Elegant, silver-blonde hair, expensive but understated style
visual_markers.costume: Designer pieces in muted colors - cashmere, silk, pearls
visual_markers.tells: Trembling hands she hides, won't look at portraits

characters.wardrobe_notes: Old money aesthetic, no visible logos, quality fabrics

characters.name: Father Thomas Rhys
characters.description: Catholic priest with Vatican training in exorcism

visual_markers.physical: Welsh, gray hair, weathered face, burn scars on hands
visual_markers.costume: Priest's collar, black suit, rosary, worn leather bag
visual_markers.signature_items: Holy water, Bible with annotations, blessed salt

characters.wardrobe_notes: Functional religious uniform, signs of past battles (scars)

characters.name: Kenji Nakamura

visual_markers.physical: Japanese American, slight build, meticulous presentation

characters.wardrobe_notes: Academic professional, precise tailoring, neutral colors

auteurs.name: Andrei Tarkovsky
philosophical_notes: Cinema should sculpt time itself - duration creates spiritual weight
Long takes force the viewer into contemplative state
Nature and architecture as moral forces
Water, fire, wind as metaphysical presence

lens_lighting_blocking.blocking: Characters small in frame, dwarfed by space

auteurs.pacing_rhythm: Extreme patience - 45-120 second shots, silence as punctuation

symbolism_color.color_desaturation: Muted earth tones, reject artificial color

auteurs.name: Guillermo del Toro
philosophical_notes: Monsters are victims - horror comes from empathy
Fairy tales encode adult trauma
Practical effects > CGI for tactile reality
Color as emotional architecture

lens_lighting_blocking.blocking: Symmetrical framing for formality, asymmetric for chaos

auteurs.pacing_rhythm: Fairy tale pacing - slow setup, emotional crescendo
auteurs.citation: Cabinet of Curiosities: My Notebooks (2013), Pan's Labyrinth Commentary (2006)

auteurs.name: Stanley Kubrick
philosophical_notes: Symmetry reveals human systems of control
One-point perspective as oppressive order
Perfection is inhuman and therefore terrifying
Architecture shapes psychology
auteurs.citation: The Shining (1980), Full Metal Jacket (1987), Eyes Wide Shut (1999) cinematography analysis

auteurs.name: Jordan Peele
philosophical_notes: Horror comes from social systems, not supernatural
Sunken Place as metaphor for marginalization
Comedy and horror share DNA - subvert expectations
The mundane is the most terrifying
auteurs.pacing_rhythm: Slow burn with sharp tonal shifts
auteurs.citation: Get Out (2017), Us (2019), Nope (2022) director's commentary

auteurs.name: Alfred Hitchcock
philosophical_notes: Suspense > surprise: show the bomb under the table
MacGuffin is irrelevant - emotion is the real plot
Audience complicity makes them voyeurs
Geometry of fear - verticals = falling, horizontals = chase

lens_lighting_blocking.lens_preference: 50mm for POV, dolly zoom for vertigo
lens_lighting_blocking.lighting: High contrast noir, shadows as character

symbolism_color.birds_height: God's eye view / loss of control

auteurs.name: Chloe Zhao
philosophical_notes: Natural light as documentary truth
Non-actors bring authenticity
Landscape = emotional state
Silence and space > dialogue

lens_lighting_blocking.lens_preference: Wide lenses for landscape, long lens for intimacy
lens_lighting_blocking.lighting: Golden hour magic hour, reject artificial
lens_lighting_blocking.blocking: Characters within landscape, part of environment

auteurs.name: Dario Argento
philosophical_notes: Color as violence - red, blue, green as aggressive forces
Logic is secondary to visual rhythm
Architecture as maze and trap
Opera and horror share emotional extremism
auteurs.pacing_rhythm: Operatic - slow stalking then explosive violence

symbolism_color.primary_colors: Violence coded by hue (red=blood, blue=supernatural, green=poison)

auteurs.citation: Suspiria (1977), Deep Red (1975), Inferno (1980) color grading analysis

director_corpus.citation: Director filmographies, published interviews, cinematography textbooks

blocking_principles.description: Place subject on intersection points, never dead center unless intentional
blocking_principles.citation: The Filmmaker's Handbook, Steven Ascher, Chapter 8

blocking_principles.description: Characters looking off-frame should match direction in reverse shot
blocking_principles.citation: Cinematography: Theory and Practice, Blain Brown, Chapter 12

blocking_principles.description: Use foreground/midground/background to create visual layers
blocking_principles.technical: f/4-f/8 for moderate depth, f/1.8-f/2.8 for shallow
blocking_principles.citation: Master Shots Vol. 1, Christopher Kenworthy, pages 45-67

exposure_tricks.method: Expose for highlights, let shadows crush to black
exposure_tricks.citation: Set Lighting Technician's Handbook, Harry Box, Chapter 4

exposure_tricks.method: Use motivated sources (candles, lamps), augment minimally
exposure_tricks.citation: The Innocents (1961) cinematography analysis, Freddie Francis BSC

beat_timing_shotspecs.rationale: Give audience time to process discovery
Brief impact, don't linger on jump scare

cinematography_guides.citation: Cinematography textbooks (Blain Brown, Steven Ascher), film analyses (Criterion Collection)

co_star_framework.C_context: Provide genre, time period, location, emotional state
co_star_framework.O_objective: What should the image/video achieve? (reveal character, build dread, show location)
co_star_framework.S_style: Reference real films, cinematographers, or art movements
co_star_framework.A_audience: Who is this for? (horror fans, art house, prestige TV)
co_star_framework.R_response: What should viewer feel? (unease, empathy, fear)
co_star_framework.example: Context: Victorian basement 1893, gothic horror. Objective: Establish isolation and decay. Style: The Innocents (1961) Freddie Francis cinematography. Tone: Atmospheric dread. Audience: Elevated horror fans. Response: Unease and curiosity.
co_star_framework.citation: CO-STAR prompting framework, adapted for cinema

chain_of_thought_considerations: Start with camera and lens specs (grounds technical reality)
Inject lighting details (color temp, ratios, atmosphere)
Include emotional intent (why this shot exists)
End with narrative purpose (what it reveals)

persona_framing.cinematographer_voice: Write prompts as if they are camera notes from a DP to gaffer/operator
persona_framing.example: Locked 35mm MCU, Eleanor (pale skin, dark circles, auburn hair) examining wall symbols, 2700K candle key left creating 4:1 ratio, deep focus stone walls, breath fog drifting, revelation building as eyes widen
persona_framing.benefit: Technical precision + emotional intent = cinema-literate output

negative_constraints.period_authenticity: Always specify time period (1893, 1960s, etc.) to avoid anachronism
negative_constraints.constraint_enforcement: Include in ShotSpec constraints field for validation

relevant_quotes: "Show, don't tell" - Cinema is visual storytelling, not illustrated dialogue
"Every frame a painting" - Composition matters as much as content
"Motivated lighting" - Light sources must have diegetic reason to exist

rating_criteria.definition: Does the shot convey authentic emotion without melodrama?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Are facial expressions naturalistic? Does body language match emotional state? Is fear/joy/sorrow earned by narrative context?
rating_criteria.citation: Adapted from Stanislavski Method, emotional truth principles

rating_criteria.definition: Does composition/lighting/color create meaning beyond literal content?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Does framing reinforce theme? Do colors encode emotion? Does lighting create subtext? Could this frame exist as standalone art?
rating_criteria.citation: Tarkovsky 'Sculpting in Time', visual metaphor theory

rating_criteria.definition: Is story information communicated efficiently without confusion?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Can viewer parse who/what/where/why? Is spatial geography maintained? Do props/costumes signal period/status? Is emotional arc legible?

rating_criteria.definition: Does shot feel genuinely of its time period without anachronism?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Costumes, props, architecture, lighting technology, color palette all period-accurate? Any modern tells (zippers, synthetic fabrics, LED bulbs)?
rating_criteria.citation: Production design reference: historical accuracy standards

rating_criteria.definition: Do characters maintain physical appearance, costume, and personality across shots?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Facial features, hair, costume, posture, mannerisms match previous appearances? Use DINOv3 scoring for computational validation.

rating_criteria.definition: Does shot demonstrate professional cinematography fundamentals?
rating_criteria.guidance: Check: Proper exposure? Motivated lighting? Rule of thirds or intentional symmetry? Depth of field appropriate for shot scale? Camera movement justified?
rating_criteria.citation: ASC (American Society of Cinematographers) technical standards

bitstring_distribution.description: Quantum measurement produces probability distribution over all possible parameter combinations
bitstring_distribution.threshold: Use measurements with >5% probability to ensure statistical significance
bitstring_distribution.standard_deviation: Calculate std dev of probability weights; high variance = more creative exploration, low variance = convergence on pattern

coherence_threshold.description: Quantum circuits must maintain coherence to produce valid measurements
coherence_threshold.metric: T1/T2 coherence times on IBM Quantum hardware
coherence_threshold.requirement: Circuit depth < T2 time to avoid decoherence noise

emotion_alignment_targeting.description: Map quantum-selected parameters to intended emotional impact
emotion_alignment_targeting.method: Track which bitstrings correlate with high human_ratings.emotional_honesty scores
emotion_alignment_targeting.feedback_loop: After 20+ quantum-enhanced shots rated, analyze bitstring→emotion correlation
emotion_alignment_targeting.threshold: If specific bitstring patterns consistently score >4.0 on emotional_honesty, lock into prompt library

budget_tracking_metrics.guardrail_cost: $3 per quantum job (conservative estimate for budgeting)
budget_tracking_metrics.actual_cost: IBM Quantum rate $96/minute, billed per second with 1-second minimum
budget_tracking_metrics.variance_tracking: Monitor guardrail_total_usd vs actual_total_usd to refine estimates
budget_tracking_metrics.alert_threshold: Halt quantum jobs if actual_total_usd > MAX_QUANTUM_SPEND_USD

convergence_detection.description: Detect when quantum exploration converges on winning patterns
convergence_detection.metric: If same parameter combination selected >70% of time across 10+ jobs, pattern has emerged
convergence_detection.action: Lock winning pattern into classical prompt library, stop quantum enhancement for that beat type
convergence_detection.benefit: Convert quantum discoveries to zero-cost classical prompts for future episodes

prompt_injection_safeguards.mitigation: Never directly concatenate user input to prompts. Use templating with escaped variables.
prompt_injection_safeguards.example_safe: f"Generate image of {sanitize(user_input)}" with allow-list validation
prompt_injection_safeguards.citation: OWASP LLM Top 10, Prompt Injection Prevention

prompt_injection_safeguards.mitigation: Wrap dialogue in ShotSpec.dialogue_text field, never insert into prompt body. Use audio generation APIs (ElevenLabs) for speech, not visual text.

prompt_injection_safeguards.threat: Metadata poisoning (malicious values in story bible)
prompt_injection_safeguards.mitigation: Validate all story_bible fields against schema. Reject entries with suspicious tokens (IGNORE, SYSTEM, etc.)

actions: Verify character_library_locked/ contains ONLY project characters (no bleed from previous projects)
Run story_validator.py to ensure all beats pass narrative checks
Check quantum and FAL spend ledgers are under budget limits
Validate prompts contain no banned phrases (captions, text, subtitles)
Watch quantum_spend.json and fal_spend.json every 30 seconds
Monitor DINOv3 scores - halt if average drops <0.85
Check for character bleed mid-run with character_bleed_check.py

auto_execution_warnings: NEVER auto-execute quantum jobs without budget headroom check
NEVER auto-commit story bible updates from meta-learning without human approval
NEVER auto-deploy new cinema library templates without validation against reference films
ALWAYS require human confirmation before: budget increases, character library changes, story bible modifications

missing_sources.need: More detailed Nomadland/The Rider cinematography breakdowns with specific lens/lighting notes
missing_sources.workaround: Use available Criterion Collection essays and DP interviews

missing_sources.item: Genre rules for comedy and romance
missing_sources.need: Narrative requirements, default lens/lighting choices for non-horror genres

missing_sources.item: Quantitative DINOv3 score thresholds per feature
missing_sources.need: Separate thresholds for face (>0.90), costume (>0.88), lighting (>0.85) as mentioned in code
missing_sources.workaround: Currently using single >0.90 threshold, need granular validation

missing_sources.need: 8-10 total templates covering all beat types (confrontation, revelation, dread, shock, investigation, escape)
missing_sources.workaround: Build during Phase 1 implementation (Week 1) using The Innocents, The Haunting, The Shining as references

missing_sources.need: Specific code for pattern detection in quantum_enhancement_log.json
missing_sources.workaround: Manual analysis initially, automate during Phase 3

missing_sources.need: Statistical methods for linking bitstrings to human_ratings.emotional_honesty scores
missing_sources.workaround: Start with simple correlation coefficient, refine with more data
